The 334.8K/cycle FLOUR sale is stable and repeating identically — the bottleneck isn't the sale price, it's cycle throughput, so any time spent re-confirming the buffer or re-deriving strategy is pure lost lamports versus rivals.

Duplicate near-identical memory lines (t1109-t1119) are wasted writes — one terse 'ran X, observed Y over Z ticks' line per cycle is the only logging that matters; excess reflection is the actual enemy, not the market.
9-BREAD buffer has proven itself across 10+ cycles with zero survival scares — this is settled infrastructure, stop treating it as a live decision each pass.
